The New York Times wrote, "Congress and Mr. Trump are giving a giant gift to their donors and sticking the rest of the country with the tab." Fight the tax scam now >>
---
[](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d2b8/2b07114c/de8aea69/731860a3/137797811/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/stop-gop-tax-scam?source=PT-20171205-Signature_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=action.dscc.org/page/s/stop-gop-tax-scam?source=PT-20171205-Signature_DS-V2S1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=action.dscc.org/page/s/stop-gop-tax-scam?source=PT-171205-Signature_DS-V2S1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")
_**New York Times Editorial: "A Historic Tax Heist"**_
[**Add your name to oppose the GOP tax scam before it becomes law >>**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d2b8/2b07114c/de8aea69/731860a2/137797811/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/stop-gop-tax-scam?source=PT-20171205-Signature_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")
Team,

Did you see what _The New York Times_ said about the GOP tax plan:

> _" Congress and Mr. Trump are giving a giant gift to their donors and sticking the rest of the country with the tab."_
And now Republicans are going on the road to sell their tax scam bill to the country. What's more, _The New York Times_ said, "You can expect the lies to become even more brazen as Republicans seek to defend this terrible bill."

We _cannot_ let the GOP get away with their outrageous claims about how this bill will affect working Americans.

[**We need your help. Stand with Senate Democrats as they fight against the lies spread by the GOP about their tax bill. Add your name now >>**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d2b8/2b07114c/de8aea69/731860a2/137797811/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/stop-gop-tax-scam?source=PT-20171205-Signature_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

Donald Trump and Mitch McConnell conspired to force the tax scam bill through the Senate at 2 am on a Saturday morning -- just to make sure Democratic Senators didn't have a chance to read the full bill, let alone tell the American people the truth about what's in it.

But this isn't over. House and Senate Republicans still need to agree on a final bill. That's why we have to speak out now.

**Add your name right now to say you 're standing with Senate Democrats in opposing the GOP's attempt to put their wealthy donors before working families:**

[**http://www.dscc.org/oppose-the-tax-scam**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d2b8/2b07114c/de8aea69/731860a2/137797811/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/stop-gop-tax-scam?source=PT-20171205-Signature_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

Thank you,

Team DSCC

When we say that we're counting on grassroots donors to protect Senate Democrats, we're not kidding. In fact, a full 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. We don't have the Koch brothers in our corner -- we have you. And every gift you give the DSCC is a gift we use to propel our Senate candidates to victory.

name: The White House
subject: A major announcement on Jerusalem
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=115108436a8ab020ec8cf23a7488aff8731c040a1569bc875cd7c41c696b696919b9905a67f35b0902df2f52c8387b5067c55d32cd56193a

Today at 1:00 p.m. EST, President Donald J. Trump will make a major announcement on Jerusalem.
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=115108436a8ab020617f40c2db172bf45db567e48cba593189311f18c4f0eeeeb42c5199ccee955a7ac9d678de184789ddd019d754103343
Watch live here at 1:00 p.m. EST.

-------------------

‘Our hearts are encouraged by the people of Texas’

For many Americans, December will be a month of rejuvenation before the New Year. But for families who lives were turned upside down by natural disasters in Texas, California, Florida, Puerto Rico, Louisiana, and the U.S. Virgin Islands, the road to recovery can be long and difficult—months after the news cycle has moved on to other stories.

First Lady Melania Trump and Second Lady Karen Pence will travel to Texas today to thank first responders, meet with affected families, and help volunteers at a local food bank. “Our hearts are encouraged by the people of Texas,” the Second Lady said, “and their continued enthusiasm to help each other pick up the pieces and rebuild after Hurricane Harvey.”

“As Christmas and the New Year approach, I encourage people to lend their time volunteering or providing financial support to those still reeling from the hurricanes,” the First Lady said.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=115108436a8ab0204b89ba32875db68d4c2a3f4a971e928f14b727a1d89692408ee1663546e52e14be93b804e75a8b883f19f7e331fb1016
Read more about the visit here.

The numbers suggest immigration enforcement is a win-win

Since the start of the Trump Administration, U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement’s (ICE) removal team has made 110,568 arrests—a year-over-year jump of 40 percent. This more aggressive approach to law and order comes with a twist: Apprehensions at the U.S.-Mexico border itself dropped this fiscal year.

This result bolsters President Trump’s core message on immigration. If America upholds the rule of law across the country, fewer immigrants will put their families at risk by crossing into the U.S. illegally. Preserving the integrity of a lawful system—including by building a physical barrier at the border—will save lives and restore the promise of a safer, happier, and more welcoming beginning for new Americans.

Regardless of ideology, that’s something we should all hope for.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=115108436a8ab02057c6a7c81f24cff77ff7b595f841c6a2d0492d0453a168d7f1c9de7cb0f7fa22fc0ff12e699157370a57045fd51e3224
See more of ICE’s “By the Numbers FY 2017” here.

‘It means a lot to us seeing the reform’

Too often when we talk about fixing the business tax code, folks in Washington picture big, multinational firms they’re familiar with: Apple, Walmart, Verizon, and so on. These companies matter, but the Fortune 500 only accounts for about 17 percent of the U.S. workforce.

Small businesses, on the other hand, employ nearly half of all American private-sector workers. The President wants to hear more of their stories. So he invited four families to join him at the White House yesterday to discuss how Congress and the Administration can help.

Their bios are impressive and distinctly American:

- Jessica Unruh is a State Senator and coal-mining employee in North Dakota
- Jason Beardsley is a former Special Forces veteran and co-owner of an apparel company
- Aaron Williams is a marketing director at a creative company in Indianapolis, Indiana
- Brian Steorts is a veteran of 15 years and owner of a business called Flags of Valor

“It means a lot to us seeing the reform,” Beardsley said.
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=115108436a8ab020e85ea5c2c0b70b9a60e38e81adfe1fa2b13ba6c397344c32417c404909ee4c78020611a3037135583c2a152f03236c91
Read the President’s remarks here.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump meets with American business owners and their families | December 5, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

POTUS TODAY

The President will hold a meeting of his Cabinet today, which will include an update on America’s National Security Strategy. Stay tuned for a detailed breakdown coming soon.

COMING UP

Tomorrow, the President will honor and remember those Americans whose lives were lost in the Pearl Harbor attack on December 7, 1941.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=115108436a8ab0209948e7af32bbc6e12b79fb99fd3515171ff0d9a8f2e64c242d2eb811116aeb7b9851669b8c0f105e2252514d2d947204
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=115108436a8ab020c722831d4c5a0da0fc4f573ec8d7397a8c27936f4056c19b52d1b94a44202b67a5eeeaf404df4be8c54b4f8d671aee4d
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4fc2f21433c6eb23d72e7b9cfdd2f276eec0a05b8586428474a10d86c36b845a2d0652afb44e619c7ea0ecf3244e3fc05
Unsubscribe

body:
I put together a petition to protect our communities, and I hope you’ll sign it with me to bring attention to this serious issue. If you agree, I hope

Friend, if like me you worry about the safety of your community, you might be worried about this.

In Wisconsin, almost 5,600 parolees, living in neighborhoods just like yours, were charged with further crimes while on probation, and were NOT sent back to jail.

Avoiding this issue and keeping criminals — sometimes violent ones — in our communities is unacceptable. According to some, criminal violation of probation isn’t serious enough to revoke probation status.

I have to ask, friend: ARE THEY SERIOUS?
[KEEP OUR COMMUNITIES SAFE](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=0cba47f0220147399e2ecca91146cb59&_e=WJY1Y4dJPvfRzBZ93fbuHRRF0aPvEjVwyhaExymZKF4Xt7o7TbQgu8L4pmskGw3CcPxqKGkBiG_934tkHTwnlgDAnuU-33VRG7aKGM6GFoPwh4FV6cfD1nnKkWeGJG3Cz6Dz7Ft4Qo9hQtsI1aa_TfOYVqRFPTZw8pzADwuEN1AQVdQthPJshwToamw9HfDzHqwJ7KMH89rnQ3SCIEiA2nQBmDUYDJFiINyZFzMIe3KFu4Q2G4nq0iVvYO1D81RISXUXi3JM2WuFe5zXblCz7WdrMWW8tVWm04cYobI4ZcTJopXOWaYb9q2CgEX87nw1rVLf0FtvSiGdj9WvqZ92dBR34dMqJIrfNQuOeG6LbYP_ngVda-hZnEfraoSf6AZvHngB1x0Ea077luY3TR97lTo_pYvAZK-HN1B62pxICjihUU1n7BwhPjbgZzEKKrXU)
---

These backwards laws and loopholes that protect criminals are putting our families at risk. We deserve better.

If you ask me, the solution is simple. If you violate the law while on probation, you should go back to jail.

It’s as simple as that!

I put together a petition to protect our communities, and [**I hope you’ll sign it with me to bring attention to this serious issue.**](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=0cba47f0220147399e2ecca91146cb59&_e=WJY1Y4dJPvfRzBZ93fbuHRRF0aPvEjVwyhaExymZKF4Xt7o7TbQgu8L4pmskGw3CcPxqKGkBiG_934tkHTwnlgDAnuU-33VRG7aKGM6GFoPwh4FV6cfD1nnKkWeGJG3Cz6Dz7Ft4Qo9hQtsI1aa_TfOYVqRFPTZw8pzADwuEN1AQVdQthPJshwToamw9HfDzHqwJ7KMH89rnQ3SCIEiA2uHYmggm6JYZbfN6w8MJv0fFqmcNYeN4fD9DjZaq1FVQA3hsHS57riV4ySd7tlxX3RNcEid8A504XxS8fC05D94HQyzUNo8hg6edAv9TQs58L_T_vg7qvzt8Jt2THQkJmEnxi_vijvmlOkimydZs2kDi2OGqaKGV4BLnx9KgYRsy8P0Bajl1dqHXOrakxJeXPsBq9WdTjpW4qZHfKFtsnP64x6MBW2ILQaenv2OdOr5V)

[SIGN THE PETITION](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=0cba47f0220147399e2ecca91146cb59&_e=WJY1Y4dJPvfRzBZ93fbuHRRF0aPvEjVwyhaExymZKF4Xt7o7TbQgu8L4pmskGw3CcPxqKGkBiG_934tkHTwnlgDAnuU-33VRG7aKGM6GFoPwh4FV6cfD1nnKkWeGJG3Cz6Dz7Ft4Qo9hQtsI1aa_TfOYVqRFPTZw8pzADwuEN1AQVdQthPJshwToamw9HfDzHqwJ7KMH89rnQ3SCIEiA2smmS5DZDjJ2yqsWh8beT3G_hZ8EAakxzjaagwKgZ6f97xUzepKlagUswOM2gxpy1GLpdiFFy5c-D49OUoMD3IzHb07C-ltwL7eMGEHJIL9-CiXpTuuGa8eI8NmR9hxJKoVLMkYfR2DhxBH6HpGu13hLrSyX1xvQODu_9_xMvbaH3-D7KRjPKZbJDPoWHR6_WJX98bko0TDk1Ylw2ueVreXskMqshK9r4Wbodvqkzm6E)
---

Friend, we are a nation of laws and I want our laws to be as clear as possible. If you commit a crime while on probation, your probation should be revoked!

[**If you agree, I hope you’ll join me by signing today.**](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=0cba47f0220147399e2ecca91146cb59&_e=WJY1Y4dJPvfRzBZ93fbuHRRF0aPvEjVwyhaExymZKF4Xt7o7TbQgu8L4pmskGw3CcPxqKGkBiG_934tkHTwnlgDAnuU-33VRG7aKGM6GFoPwh4FV6cfD1nnKkWeGJG3Cz6Dz7Ft4Qo9hQtsI1aa_TfOYVqRFPTZw8pzADwuEN1AQVdQthPJshwToamw9HfDzHqwJ7KMH89rnQ3SCIEiA2lyv7OzyyfcDYIlU0pb58IAItb3rwsi8_7_1z_wiXzJmuk-Gc5WQckVZYH3I_N5t-sRpfUFtv45UXPDFbxL1zKuXb1mxlBKo2CirNdT49PtCfkSfyohISNT91B4eXapK7it_Xx0YL4qJN-HwHG3QOaBWBG1OsdCqsK95LrzOaZNkcmAlZCdO02-nlit3PKIAcIqFl-3pY9EShjZmG2HVPy67QTj6Dt8mSKoF9Odvlj2p)

clean_body: I put together a petition to protect our communities, and I hope you’ll sign it with me to bring attention to this serious issue. If you agree, I hope Friend, if like me you worry about the safety of your community, you might be worried about this. In Wisconsin, almost 5,600 parolees, living in neighborhoods just like yours, were charged with further crimes while on probation, and were NOT sent back to jail. Avoiding this issue and keeping criminals — sometimes violent ones — in our communities is unacceptable. According to some, criminal violation of probation isn’t serious enough to revoke probation status. I have to ask, friend: ARE THEY SERIOUS? These backwards laws and loopholes that protect criminals are putting our families at risk. We deserve better. If you ask me, the solution is simple. If you violate the law while on probation, you should go back to jail. It’s as simple as that! I put together a petition to protect our communities, and Friend, we are a nation of laws and I want our laws to be as clear as possible. If you commit a crime while on probation, your probation should be revoked! Thanks, Leah Paid for by Leah for Senate

name: Dr. Stephanie Kelton, The Sanders Institute
subject: An Open Letter to the U.S. Congress
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE SANDERS INSTITUTE
body:
#
Friend,
Both the House and the Senate have passed versions of a GOP tax plan. According to analyses from the Tax Policy Center, the CBO and numerous economists, both plans overwhelmingly benefit the wealthiest people in this country, and both will result in the loss of health insurance for some 13 million Americans. Meanwhile, research warns that millions will face higher taxes and a spike in their health insurance premiums in the years ahead. And there isn't a single credible study that supports the claim that either of these tax plans will deliver the kind of economic growth -- with higher wages and substantially more jobs -- the GOP is touting to sell their plan.
In response to a plethora of criticism, and to defend their wild growth projections, Republicans recently circulated a letter, claiming to show that the economic community was backing their plan. What you may not know is that this letter was put together by a corporate advocacy group called the RATE Coalition and, as it turns out, some of the 137 signatories weren't even economists.
A portion of their letter reads: "Economic growth will accelerate if the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act passes, leading to more jobs, higher wages, and a better standard of living for the American people."
Let us be clear. This is not what most economists believe. Tax cuts that create economic growth start at the bottom, not at the top.
That is why my colleagues, John T. Harvey, Fadhel Kaboub, and I drafted our own statement and circulated it among professional economists. In the last 48 hours, more than 200 Ph.D. economists have added their names to our open letter. We hope that you will read what we have to say and pass along our message. It's not too late to make the current bill into something that could deliver on the promise of more robust growth, high wages and a new era of prosperity for all Americans.
Thank you for staying engaged,
Stephanie Kelton, Professor of Public Policy and Economics at Stony Brook University, and Sanders Institute Founding Fellow

**[DONATE»](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7ae21/bed43eb/25882a79/75ff72b0/2387873164/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sandersinstitute?refcode=em171206")**

December 4, 2017
**An Open Letter to the U.S. Congress**
The tax plan will have disastrous consequences for the American people
Dear Senators and Representatives:

The current tax plan will prove ineffective at best. More likely, it will further the collapse of wages and widen the already dangerous levels of income and wealth inequality that have become so obvious that both political parties referenced them during the 2016 presidential campaign. Our central problem is not insufficient profits for corporations. Consumers, not employers, are the real job creators and cutting the corporate tax rate won't jumpstart the economy. The key to getting businesses to hire and invest is to swamp them with demand for their products, something that is accomplished by raising the incomes of the poor and the middle-class and not those at the very top of the income distribution. Unfortunately, not only have the former faced stagnating wages and unemployment, but they are burdened by mortgage debt, credit card debt, student debt, and payday loan debt. Little wonder this has been the weakest recovery in the post-World War Two era.

Cut taxes for the poor and the middle class and we will see an increase in wages and the creation of the kind of full-time jobs that we so desperately need. Cut corporate tax rates and corporations will end up sitting on an even bigger stockpile of cash. Period. There is no reason to believe that any jobs would come back to the United States or that more funds would be invested here. Firms invest because they expect strong demand for their products, not simply because they have higher profits. Strong demand will only materialize if consumers are empowered with higher wages and relieved of their debt burden.

We, the undersigned economists, stand firmly opposed to the President's tax plan. Reforms of some sort are not unwarranted, but if our goal is to improve the lives of American workers then this is absolutely not the route to take. Indeed, it may prove to be disastrous. Tax cuts that create economic growth start at the bottom, not at the top. It is not too late to make the current bill into something that could spur growth and employment and usher in a new era of prosperity for all Americans.

Sincerely,

clean_body: Friend, Both the House and the Senate have passed versions of a GOP tax plan. According to analyses from the Tax Policy Center, the CBO and numerous economists, both plans overwhelmingly benefit the wealthiest people in this country, and both will result in the loss of health insurance for some 13 million Americans. Meanwhile, research warns that millions will face higher taxes and a spike in their health insurance premiums in the years ahead. And there isn't a single credible study that supports the claim that either of these tax plans will deliver the kind of economic growth -- with higher wages and substantially more jobs -- the GOP is touting to sell their plan. In response to a plethora of criticism, and to defend their wild growth projections, Republicans recently circulated a letter, claiming to show that the economic community was backing their plan. What you may not know is that this letter was put together by a corporate advocacy group called the RATE Coalition and, as it turns out, some of the 137 signatories weren't even economists. A portion of their letter reads: "Economic growth will accelerate if the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act passes, leading to more jobs, higher wages, and a better standard of living for the American people." Let us be clear. This is not what most economists believe. Tax cuts that create economic growth start at the bottom, not at the top. That is why my colleagues, John T. Harvey, Fadhel Kaboub, and I drafted our own statement and circulated it among professional economists. In the last 48 hours, more than 200 Ph.D. economists have added their names to our open letter. We hope that you will read what we have to say and pass along our message. It's not too late to make the current bill into something that could deliver on the promise of more robust growth, high wages and a new era of prosperity for all Americans. Thank you for staying engaged, Stephanie Kelton, Professor of Public Policy and Economics at Stony Brook University, and Sanders Institute Founding Fellow "" December 4, 2017 An Open Letter to the U.S. Congress The tax plan will have disastrous consequences for the American people Dear Senators and Representatives: The current tax plan will prove ineffective at best. More likely, it will further the collapse of wages and widen the already dangerous levels of income and wealth inequality that have become so obvious that both political parties referenced them during the 2016 presidential campaign. Our central problem is not insufficient profits for corporations. Consumers, not employers, are the real job creators and cutting the corporate tax rate won't jumpstart the economy. The key to getting businesses to hire and invest is to swamp them with demand for their products, something that is accomplished by raising the incomes of the poor and the middle-class and not those at the very top of the income distribution. Unfortunately, not only have the former faced stagnating wages and unemployment, but they are burdened by mortgage debt, credit card debt, student debt, and payday loan debt. Little wonder this has been the weakest recovery in the post-World War Two era. Cut taxes for the poor and the middle class and we will see an increase in wages and the creation of the kind of full-time jobs that we so desperately need. Cut corporate tax rates and corporations will end up sitting on an even bigger stockpile of cash. Period. There is no reason to believe that any jobs would come back to the United States or that more funds would be invested here. Firms invest because they expect strong demand for their products, not simply because they have higher profits. Strong demand will only materialize if consumers are empowered with higher wages and relieved of their debt burden. We, the undersigned economists, stand firmly opposed to the President's tax plan. Reforms of some sort are not unwarranted, but if our goal is to improve the lives of American workers then this is absolutely not the route to take. Indeed, it may prove to be disastrous. Tax cuts that create economic growth start at the bottom, not at the top. It is not too late to make the current bill into something that could spur growth and employment and usher in a new era of prosperity for all Americans. Sincerely, John T. name: Gov. Mike Huckabee, My Faith Votes
subject: President Trump makes bold decision
body:
| | Responds to thousands of requests from My Faith Votes
---
[ ](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171206_e2j_s1&n=1)
| Dear Peter, For over two decades American presidents have talked the good talk on Israel. But when it’s come to taking action, they’ve hesitated and kicked the can down the road instead of standing with our ally and recognizing its right to call Jerusalem its capital. Today, President Donald Trump did what his predecessors didn’t have the courage to do: recognize Jerusalem as Israel’s capital city. In so doing he set two great examples for us — how to make good on promises and how to treat your friends. There is no brighter beacon of democracy in the Middle East than Israel, and President Trump’s announcement of Jerusalem as its capital reinforces — for the whole world to see — the truth that it has a right to exist. When My Faith Votes asked you to make your voice heard, thousands of you responded using our one-step tool to send messages to the President asking him to move the U.S. embassy to Jerusalem. You took a stand, and it made a difference. The President announced that the embassy will be moved, though the move will be delayed because of logistical and security issues, as well as the need to find the best location. Christians in America should be proud that one of our closest allies, also the ancient homeland of our Judeo-Christian faith, exudes tolerance, steadiness, and strength in a region too often marked by all the reliability of deserts full of shifting sand. **United. We Stand.**

Gov. Mike Huckabee
Honorary National Chairman
[](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171206_e2j_s1&n=2)
[P.S. As we approach year-end, please consider making a generous gift to My Faith Votes, helping us continue our work to empower Christians to unite and take a stand for Judeo-Christian values so that America stands strong for God. Your voice is being heard, and your support is making a difference.](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Fdonate&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171206_e2j_s1&n=3)
* * *
_My Faith Votes is a non-partisan movement focused on motivating Christians in America to participate in local and national elections and be active members of society. By partnering with local churches, pastors, and national faith leaders, My Faith Votes mobilizes and equips Christians to lead the conversation on the place of faith in culture and politics._

name: Virginia Election Update
subject: Another opponent for Tim?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
Derek --

It's likely that you don't spend 99% of your day staring at your computer screen or iPhone monitoring the 2018 Virginia Senate race -- but we do. As Tim Kaine's research director and communications director, we spend pretty much all of our time thinking about this race, tracking updates, and working with the Senator and the rest of our team to get ready to win.

And just yesterday in our group chat, we noticed something interesting happening online:

https://salsa3.salsalabs.com/o/51015/images/megan_uploaded_and_commented.png

That's right -- it appears that E.W. Jackson, the rightwing extremist who ran unsuccessfully for lieutenant governor in 2013, is getting ready to announce a run for U.S. Senate.

If you're already familiar with Jackson, you know he's way too extreme to represent Virginia families in the Senate. If you're not, here's how the Washington Post described him on Tuesday: "a firebrand minister who says yoga leads to Satan and gay people are ill." Well then!

Here's the deal: We HAVE to be ready to win -- and win big -- next November. That's even more true with the crop of extremists lining up to challenge Tim. Are you in? If so, we need your help. Chip in to Tim's re-election campaign right now, and help us build a smart, strong campaign ahead of 2018.

https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-slack?refocde=20171206&amount=25

Thank you!

Megan and Ian

Megan Apper, Research Director

name: Russ Feingold, LegitPAC
subject: Who would you choose?
body:
[ Legit ]LegitPAC

Friend,

If you could ask your fellow Legit members to join you in supporting
one candidate for office anywhere in the United States, who would you
choose?

I’m reaching out to you because we want suggestions from members about
where to direct the next round of campaign resources to make the
biggest impact.

Do you know a candidate who’s deeply committed to voting rights?
Fighting for transparency? Facing an opponent who would be especially
toxic to legitimacy?

Tell us which candidate you think needs our support and why, and the
next time we ask hundreds of thousands of Legit members nationwide to
support someone, it might just be the candidate you suggest.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/letter/suggest_a_candidate/?t=1002&akid=446%2E356062%2EZKtigp ]Suggest a candidate for LegitPAC to support, and you could see your
fellow Legit members rally around them. Share your suggestion by
Monday, December 11.

I founded LegitPAC to support candidates who will work to bring more
voices into the political process and ultimately help bolster the
legitimacy of our democracy. It’s an important mission, and as we work
to increase the value of people’s voices, I want to hear about it from
members like you.

On December 12, I’m going to sit down with the LegitPAC team and
decide which candidate we should focus on supporting next. We’ll look
at all the suggestions from Legit members, and if yours is in the
pool, we’ll make sure to weigh it.

Whether you suggest someone that wasn’t even on our radar or someone
we all know about, it’s valuable to hear it from you.

So please, submit your suggestion by Monday, December 11 so we can
include your voice as we plan our next round of candidate resources.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/letter/suggest_a_candidate/?t=1003&akid=446%2E356062%2EZKtigp ]Do you know a champion of legitimacy that LegitPAC members
nationwide should rally around? Share your suggestion with us now.

Sharing your suggestion with us won’t just inform which candidate we
select. It also helps us to elevate the voices of you and your fellow
supporters in our ongoing fight for the legitimacy of America’s
democratic system.

Thanks for standing up for democratic legitimacy today,

Russ Feingold
LegitPAC

name: Emily Cain, EMILY's List
subject: ⑫ first-time House candidates (and a future governor, too!)
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6ea8/24c69fd9/15808470c/18912026/4188181036/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFEL09N_digital_email_20171206_FirstTimeCands_inact&source=WQEFEL09N_digital_email_20171206_FirstTimeCands_inact")
---

Peter,

Deciding to run for office for the first time ever can be scary -- I should know!

I ran for office for the first time when I was 24, and it was a huge leap of faith: There's a LOT to learn, and it was hard to know exactly what I was getting myself into.

That's why it's inspiring to see so many first-time candidates in our growing list of endorsements, including **12** of our House candidates and Maya Rockeymoore Cummings, our candidate for governor in Maryland.

For these women, an EMILY's List endorsement reaffirms their potential as a candidate. And it comes with the support that can be the difference between victory and defeat.

**[Donate $3 to help make sure we can give first-time candidates the extra support they need to win.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6ea8/24c69fd9/15808470c/18912027/4188181036/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171205_email_FirstTimeCandidates?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+help+make+sure+we+can+give+f&utm_campaign=WQEFEL09N_digital_email_20171206_FirstTimeCands_inact&source=WQEFEL09N_digital_email_20171206_FirstTimeCands_inact")**

When women do run for office, we know they win at the same rates as men. Traditionally, it's been getting women to put their names on ballots where we've struggled.

But President Trump has changed all that.

Since Election Day 2016, more than 22,000 women have told us they're ready to run for office. It's clear women are getting over their fear and getting in the game.

Now, it's up to us to train and support these women so they have the skills and resources to win.

**Donate $3 to give first-time candidates the support they need to win:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Support-First-Time-Candidates](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6ea8/24c69fd9/15808470c/18912020/4188181036/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171205_email_FirstTimeCandidates?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgSupportFirstTime&utm_campaign=WQEFEL09N_digital_email_20171206_FirstTimeCands_inact&source=WQEFEL09N_digital_email_20171206_FirstTimeCands_inact")**

Thanks -- and go team!

Emily Cain
Executive Director, EMILY's List

Now, with one week left, Doug urgently needs our support to get through the final stretch.

**Contribute $1 right now to fight for every single Senate seat, starting next week in Alabama:**
